description: SYNTHETIC. A ONE-TIME exercise of the fixture-promotion protocol (see tests/fixtures/PROMOTION_PROTOCOL.md), NOT a whole fake game and NOT a games/** entry. Demonstrates, end to end, how an unparsed[] taxonomy-tail line becomes a labeled unit fixture once its grammar rule lands: (a) hypothetical pre-rule state, (b) the rule as it already exists in g4's closed taxonomy, (c) the actual parse.build_events() output for that line (captured verbatim below, generated by the exercise script quoted in the protocol doc), (d) this promoted fixture itself.
taxonomy_tail_shape: triple (outcome_type 'triple') -- chosen because it does not appear anywhere in the live sample game_20260709_h94w (117 events / 5 unparsed, all 5 unparsed being the unrelated DH-pitching-sub gap), so it is a genuine 'shape not in the sample', per the g7 handoff.

step_a_hypothetical_pre_rule_unparsed.note: HYPOTHETICAL. If the 'tripled to <loc>' PRIMARY_RULES row did not yet exist, build_events() would have routed this exact line to unparsed[] with a 'no count-tail on primary clause, and clause did not match any runner rule either' reason (this is the REAL reason text build_events() emits for a line that matches no rule table at all) -- shown here for illustration only; the rule already exists (step b), so this exact miss is not reproducible from current code without deleting the rule.
step_a_hypothetical_pre_rule_unparsed.raw: Smith tripled to left field (1-2 BFK).

synthetic_input.player_table: one synthetic away batter (syn:away:1, last_name Smith) + one synthetic home player (syn:home:1, last_name Lee); see PROMOTION_PROTOCOL.md for the exact identity.PlayerTable construction

pbp_line.text: Smith tripled to left field (1-2 BFK).

events.narrative: Smith tripled to left field (1-2 BFK).

live_pending_promotion_cross_reference: The 5 real DH-pitching-sub unparsed[] lines from game_20260709_h94w are the LIVE analog of step (a) above -- they are genuinely unparsed TODAY (not hypothetical), pending the two-way-DH-rule schema gap being resolved; once that grammar/schema rule lands, they follow this SAME protocol to become a promoted fixture, exactly as this triple line does here.
